---
name: nezam-aws-infra
description: AWS CDK v2, IAM least-privilege, S3/CloudFront, and Secrets Manager patterns for production AWS deployments.
version: 1.0.0
updated: 2026-05-08
changelog: []
---

# Purpose

Specify AWS infrastructure as code with CDK v2: networking, compute, storage, IAM, secrets, and observability bindings. Single-responsibility: AWS IaC contract.

# Inputs

- Workload requirements (compute, latency, regions) from `docs/specs/`.
- API + gateway specs from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-design/SKILL.md` and `@.cursor/skills/nezam-api-gateway/SKILL.md`.
- Secrets policy from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-secret-management/SKILL.md`.
- Compliance constraints from `@.cursor/skills/nezam-privacy-compliance/SKILL.md`.

# Step-by-Step Workflow

1. Bootstrap AWS account: organizations, regions, control plane (CDK v2 + `cdk bootstrap`).
2. Define stacks: network (`VpcStack`), data (`DataStack`), compute (`AppStack`), edge (`EdgeStack`); document dependency graph.
3. Apply IAM least-privilege: per-resource scoped policies, no `*` actions, use `iam.PolicyStatement` with conditions.
4. Storage: S3 buckets with `blockPublicAccess: BLOCK_ALL`, versioning, lifecycle to Glacier; KMS-encrypted at rest.
5. CDN: CloudFront with origin failover, cache behaviors per path, OAI/OAC for private origins, security headers via Functions or Lambda@Edge.
6. Secrets: AWS Secrets Manager with rotation; pull at runtime (no env var leak); separate per environment.
7. Observability: CloudWatch metrics + alarms, X-Ray tracing, log retention policies.

# Validation & Metrics

- `cdk diff` reviewed before every apply; no unexpected resource churn.
- IAM Access Analyzer findings = 0 high severity.
- All buckets pass S3 Block Public Access; no public ACLs.
- CloudFront distributions enforce TLS 1.2+; security headers present.
- Secrets rotation enabled where supported; rotation tested.

# Output Format

- `cdk/` source (TypeScript) — stacks, constructs, tests.
- `cdk.json` and `.npmrc` configuration.
- Stack dependency map (mermaid).
- IAM policy catalogue.
- Operational runbook per stack.

# Integration Hooks

- `/DEPLOY` runs `cdk deploy` with manual approval.
- `/SCAN security` runs IAM Access Analyzer + cdk-nag.
- Pairs with `@.cursor/skills/nezam-devops-pipeline/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-secret-management/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-monitoring-observability/SKILL.md`, `@.cursor/skills/nezam-cdn-optimization/SKILL.md`.
- Honors `[.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc](.cursor/rules/workspace-orchestration.mdc)`.

# Anti-Patterns

- IAM `Action: '*'` or `Resource: '*'` without conditions.
- Hardcoded account IDs / regions in CDK source.
- Plaintext secrets in env or Parameter Store SecureString without KMS scoping.
- S3 buckets without Block Public Access.
- Skipping `cdk diff` before apply.

# External Reference

- AWS CDK v2 docs (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/v2/guide/) — current.
- AWS Well-Architected Framework (https://aws.amazon.com/architecture/well-architected/) — current.
- cdk-nag (https://github.com/cdklabs/cdk-nag) — current.
- AWS Security best practices (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/security/) — current.
- Closest skills.sh/official analog: aws-cdk / aws-iac.
